calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brianna Mannion for San Francisco Public Administrator.
John Boessenecker litigation Counsel for San Francisco Administrator.
Jerome Synold appeared by telephone for Petra Schroter (Beneficiary).

The Court discloses that Ms. Mannion is within a few months of working for the Court and in this Department as a Staff Attorney, that she did not work on this case and that it sees no reason to believe that it can not fairly adjudicate this matter. Mr. Synold stated that he is not objecting and that he is just seeking for resolution. The Court raised concern regarding the request to approve fees in excess of the amounts awarded in the ex parte petitions, stated that no further approval of fees was sought, and that this request includes fees for time spent in the process of getting hired and drafting of a letter of conflict. The Court also questions how to handle the potential conflict from the familial relationship between Janet Boessenecker and John Boessenecker, and the continual employment of Mr. Boessenecker. Ms. Mannion stated that Janet Boessenecker joined the Public Administrator in December of 2015, that only nine services were rendered since then and only pertaining to closing of escrow, that Mr. Boessenecker is just wrapping things up, that she reviewed the Civil Code of Conduct, and that this case had been handled by Mr. Ferdon, Mr. Blackman and Mr. Weinkauf at some point. Ms. Mannion also stated that Mr. Boessenecker has not been retained in any cases since Ms. Boessenecker took office, that there will be some overlapping when dealing with heir finders, but they are not being handled by Janet, that in late 2014 Mr. Boessenceker had a conversation with Mr. Ferdon about getting additional approval and that she is not sure why Mr. Ferdon waited. In addition, Ms. Mannion stated that the vast majority of fees had already been incurred, that they have not paid any of those fees except for the cost of taking Mr. Lak's deposition, and that the Public Administrator's position is that the estate benefitted and that Mr. Boessenceker should be paid. Furthermore, Ms. Mannion stated that the Public Administrator is aware of the conflict and that Mr. Boessenecker will not be getting any more work from them. Mr. Boessenecker made reference to the letter to Mr. Ferdon from 2014, provided history regarding the law suit and stated that he represented what he believed were the blood heirs. The Court stated that it does not see how preparation of the fee agreement benefitted the estate and that it was a benefit to Mr. Boessenecker getting the job. Mr. Boessenecker stated that he always bills for that. The Second and Final Account and Report is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Steven Weinkauf for Public Administrator of Estate

Mr. Weinkauf stated that he is close to filing a final accounting, his office just discovered billing for attorney they retained, and the previous attorney's ex parte petition for fees were denied. Mr. Synold stated that he is concerned about the delay and would like the matter resolved. The Status Hearing on Close of Estate is continued to July 17, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of petition for final distribution or new petition concerning status of estate requesting leave to continue administering estate.

Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights
Off Calendar Oct-19-2017 Continued To Jan-12-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessnecker for Tilo Lieder (Petitioner). Jerome Synold for Petra Schroeter (Objector).

The Court inquired whether the parties had tried to mediate this matter. Mr. Boessenecker stated that there were conversations with prior counsel, that prior counsel's client & wife passed away, and therefore mediation did not take place. Argument held. The Court directed counsel to get an independent translator for the translation of the documents. The Court set a further hearing on January 12, 2018 at 9:30 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. Matter Not Reported.

Off Calendar Aug-14-2017 Continued To Oct-19-17 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Boessenecker for Tilo Lieder.
Jerome Synold appeared by telephone for Petra Schroeter (Objector).
Steven Weinkauf for Administrator.

The Petition to Determine Inheritance Rights is continued to October 19, 2017 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204 for a hearing on the adoption issue and whether there is a share for Mr. Prieske. Mr. Boessenecker is to file his pleadings by September 11, 2017. Mr. Synold is to file his pleadings by October 2, 2017.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights
Off Calendar Jun-26-2017 Continued To Aug-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Boessenecker for Petitioner.
Steven Weinkauf for Administrator.

Mr. Boessenecker stated that Johannes Prieske was represented by Counsel (Mr. Siegwart) and then post-deceased, made reference to exhibit 22 (adoption record) in the petition, stated that Mr. Prieske is not a blood heir and made reference to Probate Code 6451 stating that the adoptive family takes the place of a blood relative when there is a foreign adoption. Mr. Boessenecker also stated that he does not know if Mr. Siegwart represents Mr. Prieske's Wife and that he can try to serve the Wife or the personal representative of his Estate in Germany. The Petition to Determine Inheritance Rights is continued to August 14, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Of Litigation
Off Calendar May-22-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator. John Boessenecker litigation Counsel for Public Administrator. John Lofton for and with Felix Laks.

Mr. Laks expressed concern about the relationship of the six individuals that were found by the heir finder, spoke of his Creditor's Claim that were rejected, and raised the issue of payments he is making for insurance and license fees for the Decedent's vehicle. Mr. Ferdon explained that the next phase will be to determine heirship and raised the issue of bills not being turned over to Mr. Watson. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Settlement of Litigation (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Jones. (Not Reported)

Mr. Ferdon ascerts priorty to serve on behalf of the Public Administrator and stated that there may be other relatives. The Court appointed the Public Administrator as the Administrator of the Estate of Ursulla Lieder (subject to receipt of the order from Mr. Ferdon).

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-29-2011 Continued To Aug-24-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Payette for and with Feliz Laks (Petitioner). Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Payette and Mr. Laks spoke of the Decedent's intent, the 35 year relationship between the Decedent and Mr. Laks, the Decedent and Mr. Laks purchasing a house together, Mr. Laks being considered like a putative Spouse and his creditor's claim. Mr. Ferdon stated that he was here to assert the Public Administrator's priorty and once appointed would see that the Brother of the Decedent is aware of all of the issues. The matter was continued to August 24, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Payette to contact the Decedent's Brother.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
